steps.title: Pin down what the claim requires
steps.content: The claim has two parts: Odalie finds her everyday life monotonous, AND she is eager to escape it. The right quotation must show both the dullness of her routine and her readiness for something new.

steps.title: Test the strongest candidate
steps.content: Choice C says "life becomes a dull thing, and one is ready for any new sensation." The first half ("dull thing") establishes the monotony; the second half ("ready for any new sensation") establishes her eagerness to escape it. Both halves of the claim are illustrated in a single sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the others by what they miss
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Mardi Gras was a tiresome day, after all': this is Odalie after the carnival, dismissing the escape as boring — it contradicts the "eager to escape" half of the claim. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In the old French house... lived Odalie in convent-like seclusion': this describes her secluded setting but says nothing about her wanting out — misses the eagerness half. ✗
  • Choice C — 'life becomes a dull thing, and one is ready for any new sensation': names the monotony and the eagerness in one breath. ✓
  • Choice D — 'early through her window Odalie could hear the jingle of folly bells': depicts the carnival sounds reaching her but doesn't establish either the monotony or her eagerness — overreach to call this an illustration of the claim. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
